session_note: S56 -- user-approved batch ('approved! work thoroughly the top recommended priorities', 2026-05-30) after a cross-network playtest bug report. Standard-tier 2-priority batch. P1 (URGENT) client AttractDrag self-view parity: P2 client saw a drag-attracted free spark frozen at spawn then teleport on release. GAP1 applyPerSubstep->stepAttractLerp only inside stepPhysics (!isClient-gated main.ts:454); GAP2 applySnapshotCore rebuilds freeSparks at spawn pos every 10Hz while the S52 dragLock guarded only interpolatePositions. FIX Arch1: client runs applyPerSubstep xPHYSICS_SUBSTEPS in the tick loop + interpolateInto preserves the drag-locked spark across applyNetSnapshot. Opponent-view S52-d6 DEFERRED. P2 anti-bloat per-file LOC trim (audioManager.ts 982 + main.ts ~921), behavior-preserving; code-health not bundle KB.
council_battle_ledger: 3-way Council R1 (Grok grok-4.20-reasoning t0.3 + Gemini gemini-2.5-pro t0.3), both REVISE, core Arch1 validated. CONVERGENT (Grok#4+Gemini A/D): mid-drag spark vanish/restate must not crash -- pre-mitigated by applyPerSubstep null/!Free guard (controls.ts:204) + guarded restore; REVISE met by explicit guards + interruption unit tests. ADOPT Arch1 (reuse host applyPerSubstep) over Arch2; preserve/restore over making applyNetSnapshot dragLock-aware. REFUTED w/ evidence: Grok#3 cadence-overshoot (dtSec=min(.,0.05) clamp main.ts:449 -> <=3 ticks/frame, same loop); Grok#6b prevPos-pop (client runs no verlet). DEFER: thin-predicted-world, rejected-placement flash, network-chaos replay harness, opponent-view S52-d6. SHIP after guards+tests. Full ledger in PDR.
prime_audit: PRIME-AUDIT + Runtime-Verifiability: no rubber-stamp (both REVISE). Convergent BLOCKER/HIGH (mid-drag vanish) pre-mitigated by existing guards -> no crash. Ordering verified: prediction in tick-loop (pre-render), preserve in render-phase interpolateInto captures the just-predicted pos, no double-lerp. 300ms pendingPlaceFromFree TTL: getDragLockedSparkId returns id through TTL so preserve extends to post-release window then null. Runtime-Verifiability met by Sym G real-peer e2e + 4 unit guards. EOS: GitHub Actions billing still blocks CI/Deploy (user action).

priorities.label: S56 P1 -- client AttractDrag self-view prediction (Player-2 parity bug: dragged spark frozen at spawn + teleport on release)
priorities.check_method: P1 verified end-to-end (Runtime-Verifiability, applying the S55 #half-wired-latch lesson). ROOT CAUSE airtight: applyPerSubstep->stepAttractLerp (cursor-follow) called ONLY inside stepPhysics (physicsLoop.ts:83), which is !isClient-gated (main.ts:454) -> client never produced the follow (GAP1); and applySnapshotCore clears+rebuilds freeSparks at snapshot/spawn pos every 10Hz (save.ts:378,419-433) while the S52 dragLock guarded only interpolatePositions not the rebuild -> a predicted spark would reset to spawn 10x/s (GAP2). FIX: (a) main.ts client branch runs controls.applyPerSubstep() xPHYSICS_SUBSTEPS(8) inside the accumulator-bounded tick loop (dtSec clamp 0.05 -> <=3 ticks/frame = matches host, no overshoot); (b) ClientSync.interpolateInto preserves the drag-locked spark pos+prevPos across applyNetSnapshot, restore guarded on still-present+still-Free. VERIFICATION: unit 871->875 GREEN (+4 sync tests: predicted pos survives a resetting snapshot; WITHOUT-lock witnesses the reset bug; spark-absent -> no throw + drag ends; spark-not-Free -> restore skipped, no throw -- the Council Gemini-A/D interruption guards). tsc -b clean. Build OK, bundle 499.61->499.99 KB (0.01 KB under the 500 KB soft target; flagged -- extraction won't reclaim it). NEW e2e Sym G (real 2-peer WebRTC): joiner presses a spawn-zone spark, drags toward (1500,400) WITHOUT releasing; held-state poll confirms the joiner freeSpark tracked the cursor to within 150px of target AND >300px from spawn-center (pre-fix this times out, frozen at spawn) -- PASS 15.0s. Full smoke regression 9 pass / 1 skip (Sym E placeholder): baseline, Sym A place, Sym C bond, Sym D color-seg, Sym F territory, Sym I win, protocol-mismatch x2 all GREEN -> zero regression on the client-interpolation paths the fix touches. Commit e51124e (NOT pushed -- GitHub Actions billing still blocks CI/Deploy; push deferred to post-billing so the deploy runs).

priorities.label: S56 P2 -- anti-bloat per-file LOC trim (audioManager.ts 982 + main.ts ~921, behavior-preserving extraction)
priorities.deferral_reason: User-approved defer (2026-05-30). Empirical finding: build sits at 499.99/500 KB and extraction ADDS ~0.3 KB module overhead (S19 precedent), so the planned LOC-trim would push the bundle OVER the soft cap it exists to protect. Extraction helps per-file LOC (§XV) but is bundle-neutral-to-positive; only code DELETION reclaims KB, and the codebase was dead-code-audited 3x (S30/33/34) -> low yield. Carry-forward: a dedicated dead-code/dependency-deletion pass when a feature actually needs headroom, OR raise the soft cap; NOT a blind extraction at-cap.

reflexion_entries_to_archive: S56 #client-prediction-was-never-run-not-just-clobbered -- the Player-2 'dragged shape frozen at spawn then teleports' bug was a TWO-gap half-wired mechanism, mirroring S55's errorLatched. The S52 dragLock (skip the dragged spark in snapshot interpolation) was built to PRESERVE a client-side AttractDrag prediction -- but the prediction was never PRODUCED: applyPerSubstep (stepAttractLerp) lived only inside stepPhysics, which is host/solo-gated, so the client never ran it (GAP1). And even once produced, applySnapshotCore's clear+rebuild resets the dragged spark every 10Hz because the dragLock only guarded interpolatePositions, not the full apply (GAP2). Lesson: when a guard/preserve mechanism exists but the symptom persists, check whether the value it guards is actually being PRODUCED end-to-end -- a preserve around a never-generated value is invisible to unit+tsc+Council; only the real-peer E2E proves the produce+preserve chain. Fix reused the host's exact applyPerSubstep (identical feel, zero dup) + completed the dragLock with a preserve/restore around applyNetSnapshot. Verified by a new 2-peer e2e (Sym G) asserting the held-drag spark tracks the cursor + 4 unit guards.

S56 #anti-bloat-extraction-breaches-the-cap-it-protects -- P2 (anti-bloat trim) DEFERRED after empirical discovery: the build sits at 499.99/500 KB and extraction ADDS ~0.3 KB module overhead (S19 precedent), so the planned trim would push the bundle OVER the soft cap it exists to protect. 'Anti-bloat trim' conflates two metrics -- per-file LOC (§XV, extraction helps) vs shipped bundle KB (only deletion helps). At-cap, extraction is counterproductive. Real headroom = a dedicated deletion/dependency pass when a feature needs it, or raise the soft cap. Lesson: verify which metric a 'trim' actually moves before executing -- the handoff's 'trim main.ts for headroom' framing was a category error (LOC vs KB), surfaced at session start and confirmed at-cap here.

SESSION #s56-shipped-p1-attract-drag-parity-deferred-p2 -- P1 (URGENT playtest bug) client AttractDrag self-view prediction SHIPPED + verified (commit e51124e; unit 871->875; new 2-peer e2e Sym G joiner-follow + full smoke 9pass/1skip; tsc clean; bundle 499.61->499.99 KB). Two-gap half-wired fix: the client never ran applyPerSubstep (stepPhysics is host-gated) AND the S52 dragLock didn't shield applySnapshotCore's 10Hz rebuild. 3-way Council (Grok+Gemini both REVISE->addressed); PRIME-AUDIT refuted 2 Grok claims (cadence-overshoot, prevPos-pop) with evidence. P2 (anti-bloat) DEFERRED -- extraction would breach the at-cap bundle. NOT pushed: GitHub Actions billing still blocks CI/Deploy (user action pending) -> P1 verified locally, not yet live. Context ~33% GREEN. API ~$0.04.
